description: Shared types for the dev.idiolect.* Lexicon family. Covers (a) cross-cutting reference shapes (lens/schema/encounter refs, tool identity, visibility), and (b) content-theory types (purpose, lensProperty, evidence, caveat) shared across multiple records. Record-specific combinator trees (condition, eligibility, constraint, convention) live in their respective record lexicons.

visibility.description: Visibility scope for a record. community-scoped semantics are reserved but deferred for v1; records with visibility=community-scoped must not be served to parties outside the named community once the substrate supports scope enforcement.

schemaRef.description: Reference to a schema. Either an at-uri pointing to a schema record or a content-addressed hash; at least one must be present.

uri.description: AT-URI pointing to a schema record, when addressable via atproto.

cid.description: Content-addressed hash of the schema, when tracked in a content-addressed store such as panproto-vcs.

lensRef.description: Reference to a lens (translation). Either an at-uri or a content-addressed hash; at least one must be present.

uri.description: AT-URI pointing to a lens record.

encounterRef.description: Reference to a prior encounter record. at-uri is canonical; cid pins the exact revision.

tool.description: Identity and version of a tool used by a verifier or observer.

commit.description: Optional source commit or build identifier.

vocabRef.description: Reference to a community-published action vocabulary. Determines how 'action' identifiers are resolved and subsumed.

uri.description: AT-URI pointing to the vocabulary record.

cid.description: Content hash pinning a specific vocabulary revision.

use.description: Compound 'what was done, on what material, for what end, by which actor.' ThUse content-theory shape, reused across records whose structured subject is an action performed, desired, endorsed, or prohibited.

action.description: Action identifier, resolved against `actionVocabulary` (or the implicit default when omitted). Examples: 'train_model', 'annotate', 'archive'.

purpose.description: The end the action serves, resolved against `purposeVocabulary` when present. Examples: 'commercial', 'non_commercial', 'academic', 'any_purpose'.

actor.description: Who ultimately performs or benefits from the action. Examples: 'researchers', 'students', 'production_users'.

actionVocabulary.description: Vocabulary the `action` identifier resolves against. Omit to use a consumer-chosen default.

purposeVocabulary.description: Vocabulary the `purpose` identifier resolves against. Omit to use a consumer-chosen default.

materialSpec.description: Description of the material a purpose operates on. scope is a community-defined tag; uri optionally pins a specific corpus or dataset.

uri.description: Optional pointer to the specific dataset or corpus.

lensProperty.description: What a verification actually asserts. Each verification kind has its own statement shape. Consumers (proof checkers, PBT runners, conformance runners) dispatch on the tagged variant.

lpRoundtrip.description: Roundtrip domain: the input set across which lens ∘ unlens is claimed to be the identity.

domain.description: Symbolic description of the input set (e.g. 'all valid UD-2.14 sentences', 'records with lengths ≤ 1024').

generator.description: Optional pointer to a generator that enumerates the domain.

lpGenerator.description: Property-based test generator spec: what the PBT runner should sample.

spec.description: Generator specification. Format is generator-runner-specific; may be a proptest Strategy reference, a Hypothesis strategy, or a QuickCheck Arbitrary.

lpTheorem.description: Formal theorem statement: the property expressed in a proof-assistant vocabulary.

statement.description: The theorem statement, verbatim, in the declared `system` syntax.

freeVariables.description: Names of free variables the statement quantifies over.

lpConformance.description: Conformance standard identifier. A conformance runner matches on name + version, optionally narrowed to named clauses.

clauses.description: Optional subset of the standard's clauses the verification covers.

lpChecker.description: Static-check configuration: which checker and which ruleset the verification covers.

ruleset.description: Named ruleset, configuration preset, or lint family.

lpConvergence.description: Convergence claim: a property preserved under repeated application of the lens, appropriate for fixed-point or reconciliation lenses.

property.description: Symbolic name or description of the preserved property.

boundSteps.description: Optional bound on steps to reach fixpoint.

evidence.description: Structured evidence for a retrospection finding. Complements the narrative `detail` field on finding; kind=other may omit evidence entirely.

evidenceDivergence.description: For kind=merge-divergence: two translation paths that should have converged but produced different outputs.

pathA.description: Sequence of lenses composed in path A.

pathB.description: Sequence of lenses composed in path B.

witnessInput.description: Optional cid of an input where the two paths diverge.

evidenceLoss.description: For kind=data-loss: a source-schema field unrepresented in the target after translation.

sourceField.description: Dotted path into the source schema identifying the lost field.

targetSchema.description: Target schema under which the loss was observed.

evidenceMismatch.description: For kind=reconciliation-mismatch: two records that should reconcile under the lens but do not.

expectedEqualityOn.description: Dotted path or projection under which equality was expected.

caveat.description: Structured caveat on a recommendation: a known failure mode consumers can match on. Paired with `caveatsText` on the record for narrative context.

affects.description: Dotted paths or field names the caveat applies to.

basis.description: Structured grounding for an attitudinal claim: what the holder's assertion/endorsement/belief rests on. Useful when the record's author is not the holder (third-party attestation) or when the grounds are externally anchored.

basisSelfAsserted.description: The holder is asserting directly, with no external grounding claimed. This is the default when a record carries no `basis`.

basisCommunityPolicy.description: Grounded in a community's published policy. `community` is the community record; `policyUri` optionally names the specific policy document.

community.description: AT-URI of the community record whose policy grounds this record.

policyUri.description: Optional pointer to the specific policy document.

basisExternalSignal.description: Grounded in something outside ATProto — a public license, an external policy, a standards document, a statement on another network.

signalType.description: Short identifier for the signal kind (e.g. 'license:CC-BY-NC-4.0', 'robots.txt', 'policy:iso-8601').

description.description: Optional narrative of how the signal grounds the claim.

basisDerivedFromRecord.description: Grounded in another record on ATProto. `source` pins the exact version via strong ref; `inferenceRule` names how this record derives from it (e.g. 'translates', 'aggregates', 'classifies').

source.description: Strong reference (AT-URI + CID) to the record this claim is derived from.

strongRecordRef.description: Strong reference to an ATProto record: AT-URI plus CID. Parallel to com.atproto.repo.strongRef but repeated here so the defs tree is self-contained.
